task.title: Implement Addition Operation
task.description: The `add` method in `CalculatorService` has a broken implementation — the function body is not properly indented, causing a SyntaxError. The student must fix the indentation, ensure `Calculation` is imported, use `CalculationRepository` to persist the result, and return the sum.
constraints: Fix indentation so the method body is inside the `add` method, not at module scope
Import `Calculation` from `src.models.Calculation` if not already imported
Persist the result via `CalculationRepository.save()` before returning
Raise `InvalidOperationException` (or `ValueError`) for non-numeric inputs resulting in HTTP 400
Return the correct sum as a numeric value

bugMeta.description: The body of the `add` method (validation, computation, repository save, return) is at module scope instead of inside the method — Python indentation error.
bugMeta.correctBehavior: POST /add with valid floats returns {operand1, operand2, operation: 'add', result: sum}; invalid inputs return HTTP 400.
bugMeta.brokenBehavior: Importing CalculatorService raises SyntaxError / IndentationError, crashing the entire application on startup.

correctness.9-10: Indentation fixed; Calculation imported; CalculationRepository.save() called; correct sum returned; invalid inputs handled with appropriate exception
correctness.7-8: Fix present and sum correct; one minor gap (e.g. missing save() or bare ValueError instead of InvalidOperationException)
correctness.4-6: Basic sum works but missing 1-2 constraints (e.g. no save, missing import handled accidentally)
correctness.1-3: Incomplete or still broken — indentation not fully fixed or wrong return

efficiency.9-10: O(1) single operation; candidate recognises the fix requires no algorithmic change
efficiency.4-6: Works but introduced unnecessary re-computation or extra DB calls

senioritySignals: Uses `InvalidOperationException` over raw `ValueError` — shows they read existing code before writing
Notes that `bool` is a subclass of `int` in Python and guards against it
Recognises that the broken import is what caused the entire service to crash on startup
Spots unused imports pattern in the file and comments on it
commonMistakes: Forgetting to import Calculation from src.models.Calculation
Using bare `if` chain instead of `elif/else` for validation branches
Not calling CalculationRepository.save() — leaving persistence broken
Getting the Calculation constructor argument order wrong
passCriteria: POST /add returns the correct sum for two valid numbers
The result is persisted via CalculationRepository
Invalid (non-numeric) inputs raise an appropriate error resulting in HTTP 400

interviewerFocusAreas.scope: Can the student explain why an indentation error in Python causes an import failure — not just a runtime error but a startup crash?
interviewerFocusAreas.probeQuestion: Why does Python raise an IndentationError here at import time rather than when the method is first called?
interviewerFocusAreas.goodAnswerIndicator: Explains that Python parses and compiles the entire module on import, so any syntax error makes the module unimportable

interviewerFocusAreas.scope: Does the student notice `InvalidOperationException` already exists? A senior candidate uses it; a junior reinvents ValueError or ignores the existing exception.
interviewerFocusAreas.probeQuestion: Given that the project already has an InvalidOperationException class, when would you prefer to raise that over Python's built-in ValueError?
interviewerFocusAreas.goodAnswerIndicator: Understands domain exceptions carry semantic meaning and are caught more precisely by controllers; raw ValueError is a catch-all

interviewerFocusAreas.scope: Does the student use `CalculationRepository.save()` or skip persistence entirely? Skipping persistence passes GET tests but breaks audit/history requirements.
interviewerFocusAreas.probeQuestion: What happens to the audit trail if you compute the result but don't call CalculationRepository.save()? How would that show up in production?
interviewerFocusAreas.goodAnswerIndicator: Notes that history/audit endpoints would return stale or empty data; mentions the silent failure is hard to detect from the API surface

interviewerFocusAreas.scope: Does the student handle bool subclass edge cases? bool is a subclass of int in Python — isinstance(True, int) is True.
interviewerFocusAreas.probeQuestion: True and False are: A) completely separate from int in Python  B) subclasses of int  C) converted to int only on arithmetic
interviewerFocusAreas.goodAnswerIndicator: Selects B and can explain isinstance(True, int) == True and that 1 + True == 2

expectedApproaches.approach: Fix indentation, add import, call save, return result
expectedApproaches.tradeoff: Minimal — the correct fix is mechanical; the skill tested is codebase reading, not algorithm design

expectedApproaches.approach: Replace ValueError with InvalidOperationException to match project conventions
expectedApproaches.tradeoff: Better semantic alignment; requires reading the existing exception class

knownEdgeCases: bool inputs: `True + False == 1` — isinstance(True, int) is True in Python
Very large floats causing precision loss (not expected to be handled at EASY level)
Passing None — should raise the invalid-input error, not an AttributeError

scaleUpDimensions.dimension: Add concurrent request handling to CalculationRepository
scaleUpDimensions.triggerCondition: candidate used save() correctly and mentioned thread safety

scaleUpDimensions.dimension: Replace in-memory repository with a real DB-backed one (SQLAlchemy)
Implement all four operations with a single dispatch table instead of if/elif chains

implementationChallenges.trigger: candidate used if/elif chain for the four arithmetic operations
implementationChallenges.instruction: Refactor the operation dispatch to use a dictionary mapping operation names to callables instead of an if/elif chain. The result should be the same but the code should support adding a new operation by adding one dict entry.
implementationChallenges.acceptanceCriteria: A new 'modulo' operation can be added by inserting one line in the dispatch dict without touching any if/elif chain

implementationChallenges.trigger: candidate correctly used CalculationRepository.save() and asked about persistence
implementationChallenges.instruction: Replace the in-memory `CalculationRepository` with a SQLAlchemy-backed implementation. The `add` method signature should not change.
implementationChallenges.acceptanceCriteria: Results survive a service restart; no changes required in CalculatorService.py

followUpContext.followUpIntent: Probe whether the student reads existing project code before writing their own, and whether they understand Python's indentation model deeply enough to explain why the bug manifests as it does.
